name: Model-Based Testing

x.defectClass: Behavioural / state-transition bugs revealed by comparing an implementation against an explicit behavioural model.
x.oracle: A formal/behavioural model (state machine, spec) from which both test cases and expected outputs are derived.
x.automationLevel: High — tools generate test sequences and oracles from the model.
blindSpots: Bugs in the model propagate to the tests
Behaviour outside the model is untested
x.cost: High — building and maintaining the model is the investment.
x.lifecycleStage: Design / system testing of protocols, embedded, and stateful systems.

def: Deriving test cases and their expected results automatically from an explicit behavioural model (often a state machine) of the system.
problem: Hand-writing test cases for complex stateful behaviour is incomplete and misses transition combinations.
mechanism: Engineers build a model of intended behaviour (states, transitions, guards). A tool traverses the model to generate input sequences and the expected outputs (the oracle), then runs them against the implementation and reports divergence.
failures: Over-large models that cannot be traversed
whenNot: No stable behavioural spec to model
myths: "The model guarantees correctness" — it only checks the implementation against the model, which may be wrong
